headline: MGM’s $18B Bid Proves Las Vegas Sands Is Undervalued
summary: Investors often mistake a declining stock chart for a broken business, over-penalizing short-term operational hurdles while ignoring the immense value of an irreplaceable physical footprint. That structural mispricing was laid bare on June 1, when People Incorporated launched an $18 billion buyout proposal to take MGM Resorts (MGM) private. The bid sent an institutional-grade signal to the market: public equities are severely undervaluing premium physical gaming moats. [1] The core thesis relies
